person.name: Thomas Hughson
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Family History
Note: Thomas came from England or Scotland about 1690 and settled at Dobbs Ferry, on the east bank of the Hudson River, in the Philipsburg Manor in Westchester County, in the colony of New York.Little is known of his origins except for traditions passed down in various branches of the family.

One story, written down by John Ward Hughson in 1964 is that the Hughson's were Scottish, and came to America via England. "The Hughson family originally was Scottish, of the Clan of Donald, of the Isles. Some of the family left the native hearth to settle near Chester, south of Liverpool. Then some left Chester and settled first on Staten Island. Finding that place too windy, they moved inland up the Hudson River...."

Another story, passed on by Edward Byron Huson is that the first Hughson immigrant was the son of an "English Lord" who bequeathed money to his son in America which was never claimed because father and son took sides in some conflict involving the British.

Still another legend, told by Hobart Huson, Sr. is that the Hughson's were french Huguenots (Calvinist Protestants) who fled to England following the the revocation of the Edict of Nantes (1685) and thence to New York.

Regardless of these stories, it seems clear that Thomas Hughson was an English-speaking immigrant who gave English names to all of his children.

Thomas Hughson and Maria Dobbs may have met and married at Flatlands, Long Island.
Why Thomas Hughson was referred to in an old commissoners report as the "Earle of Warwick alias Thomas Hughson" is unknown, but may attest to his English birth. Thomas Hughson's death is unreported, but probably occurred soon after 1741.

Thomas son, John, and his wife Sara, were hung.
Thomas, along with son's, Richard, Walter, and William, were incarcerated in the West Chester County Jail, were they remained over the summer of 1741. As there was no evidence against them, the men pleaded with the judicial system to free them on the basis that their families were starving and needed their support. Eventually they were released with the stipulation that they would leave the county immediately.

http://www.gencircles.com/users/de bbieann/1/data/111
Note: Earl of Warwick?
http://trees.ancestry.com/rd?f=document&guid=4466b39f-f102-4fcc-a5d8-248f49f6ceb7&tid=7179083&pid=-1147853378
Note: More about Thomas Hughson
http://trees.ancestry.com/rd?f=document&guid=f93ce086-f451-4bd1-90fd-c05c91b55eb5&tid=7179083&pid=-1147853378
Note: Huge amount of content about Thomas Hughson
http://trees.ancestry.com/rd?f=document&guid=f64a077d-5c84-4431-8668d655cdedb88d&tid=7179083&pid=-1147853378
Note: Hughson family history
http://trees.ancestry.com/rd?f=document&guid=64adff33-2412-4cf6-bd8c-f2615f6d6d2e&tid=7179083&pid=-1147853378
Maria Dobbs m. circa 1705 11The Earl of Warwick" Thomas Hughson and settled on Philipse Manor on a leasehold near her brother.
MARIA DOBBS, daughter of Walter Dobbs and his wife, Mary Merritt, of Barren Island, (Flatlands) married circa 1705 The Earl of Warwick., Thomas Hughson, (Road Commissioners Records, 1723, pp. 4-7 - "The Albany Post Road" - County Clerk's Office, White Plains, N.Y.) and settled on a leasehold of 279 acres north of that of her brother, John. Their leasehold was located at the present intersection of Broadway and Ashford Avenue in Dobbs Ferry - between the 329 acre leasehold of Thomas Storm to the north, and the 220 acre leasehold of Joseph Conklin, to the south.
Maria Dobbs Hughson had these four children in exactly two years. Her name disappears from the Church Records after May 25 & 26, 1714 when she sponsored the baptism of her nephew, Thomas Dobbs, son of her brother, John. Maria apparently died circa 1716 because on April 23, 1717, her widower, Thomas Hughson, and his wife, Christiena ---- had their son, Georg. bp. at the Sleepy Hollow Church.

sourceList.title: Thomas Hussen in entry for Richard Hussen, "New York, Church Records, 1660-1954"

variants.name: Huson
meanings: English: patronymic from the personal name Hugh, Hue (see Hugh ) + -son.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

historicEvents.title: Gregorian Calendar is Adopted
historicEvents.description: Gregorian calendar was adopted in England in 1752. That year, Wednesday, September 2, 1752, was followed by Thursday, September 14th, 1752, which caused the country to skip ahead eleven days.

historicEvents.title: Seven Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Seven Years' War began as a North American conflict then stretched between England and France. England, along with allies, battled France in America, India, and Europe, making it arguably the first global war. The conflict ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763 and England was victorious. The Seven Years' war ultimately led to discontent in the colonies and the American Revolution.

historicEvents.title: Boston Tea Party
historicEvents.description: Thousands of British troops were sent to Boston to enforce Britain's tax laws. Taxes were repealed on all imports to the American Colonies except tea. Americans, disguised as Native Americans, dumped chests of tea imported by the East India Company into the Boston Harbor in protest. This escalated tensions between the American Colonies and the British government.

historicEvents.title: The Shot Heard Around the World
historicEvents.description: "On April 18, 1775, a shot known as the ""shot heard around the world"" was fired between American colonists and British troops in Lexington, Massachusetts. This began the American War for Independence. Fifteen months later, Thomas Jefferson penned the Declaration of Independence. The Treaty of Paris was signed in September 1783 which ended the war. The colonies were no longer under British rule. Many who fought for the British fled to Canada, the West Indies, and some to England."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hughson was christened on 17 May 1668, in Acton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Magness Hughson, was 18 and his mother, Janet Mouat, was 17. He married Maria Merritt DOBBS on 27 July 1694, in Flatlands, Kings, New York Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Acton, Cheshire, England in 1668.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hughson è stato battezzato il 17 maggio 1668 nel Acton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Magness Hughson, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Janet Mouat, aveva 17 anni. ko.Flatlands, Kings, New York Colony, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 킨그스 플라틀랜드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hughson(이)가 1668년 5월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 악턴에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Magness Hughson(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Mouat(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1694년 7월 27일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 킨그스 플라틀랜드즈에서 Maria Merritt DOBBS(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1668년도에 영국 체실르 악턴에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hughson foi batizado em 17 de maio de 1668, em Acton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Magness Hughson, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Janet Mouat, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria Merritt DOBBS em 27 de julho de 1694, em Flatlands, Kings, New York Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Acton, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1668.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hughson был крещен 17 мая 1668 г. в местности актон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Magness Hughson было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Mouat, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Merritt DOBBS 27 июля 1694 г. в местности флатландс, кингс, ню ерк колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности актон, чешир, Англия в 1668 году.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Hughson se bautizó el 17 de mayo de 1668, en Acton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Magness Hughson, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Janet Mouat, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Maria Merritt DOBBS el 27 de julio de 1694, en Flatlands, Kings, New York Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Acton, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1668.
